NAME: SAGE-net
FULL NAME: scalable framework for sequence-to-function predictions from personal genomes
DESCRIPTION: A scalable framework and software package for training and evaluating sequence-to-function (S2F) deep learning models using personal genomes. Personal genome training improves gene expression prediction for held-out individuals, but gains arise from identifying predictive variants rather than learning a cis-regulatory grammar that generalizes across loci.
MANUAL_CITATION: Spiro, A. E. et al. A scalable approach to investigating sequence-to-function predictions from personal genomes. Nat. Methods (2026).
TITLE: A scalable approach to investigating sequence-to-function predictions from personal genomes.
Authors: Spiro AE,Tu X,Sheng Y,Sasse A,Hosseini R,Chikina M,Mostafavi S
ABSTRACT: Sequence-to-function (S2F) models can evaluate arbitrary DNA sequences, yet they struggle to fully capture inter-individual variation in gene expression. We introduce SAGE-net, a scalable framework for training and evaluating S2F models using personal genomes. While personal genome training improves gene expression prediction accuracy for held-out individuals, performance gains arise primarily from identifying predictive variants rather than learning a cis-regulatory grammar that generalizes across loci. Scalable software will be critical to advancing S2F models for personal genomics.
CITATION: Spiro AE, Tu X, Sheng Y, ...&, Mostafavi S. (2026) A scalable approach to investigating sequence-to-function predictions from personal genomes. Nat Methods, () . doi:10.1038/s41592-026-03124-8. PMID 42260311
